delta.text: Found the bug. In `body()`, when parsing a method declaration (
`function o:get()`), the C code calls `new_localvarliteral(ls, "self")` to add a `self` parameter to
`actvar` before `adjustlocalvars(ls, 1)`. The Rust port has that call commented out,
so `adjust_local_vars` tries to read `actvar[firstlocal + 0]` which doesn't exist.

The Rust port has that call commented out, so `adjust_local_vars` tries to read `actvar[firstlocal + 0]` which doesn't exist.

— the expected value. No instrumentation was added, so nothing to remove.

- `
crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:3024` — in `body()`, when parsing a method declaration (`function
o:get()`), the C call `new_localvarliteral(ls, "self")` was comm
ented out as a `TODO(port)`. `adjust_local_vars(ls, 1)` then indexed `actv
ar[firstlocal + 0] = actvar[1]` on a

`Vec` of length 1, producing the `index out of bounds` panic.
- A second bug down
stream: `:` method invocation in `suffixedexp` had `luaK_self` st
ubbed out, so the `funcargs` assertion `f.k == ExprKind::NonRe
loc` fired (the callee was still `VKStr`, never

lowered through OP_SELF).

**Fix:**
- Intern `b"self"` via `state.intern_

str` and call `new_local_var` before `adjust_local_vars(ls, 1)` in `body()`.
- Implemented `cg_self
` (the C `luaK_self`) in `lua-parse`: discharges receiver to a register, re
serves 2 regs for func+self, emits `OP_SELF` with the method name as a `K`
-bit constant, and leaves `e` as `NonReloc` pointing at the base register. Wired it into the `:` arm of `suffixedex

p`.

**New state:** The test program now prints `10` and exits cleanly with `status=Ok`.

content.text:
Output is `10` — the expected value. No instrumentation was added, so nothing to remove.

**Root cause:**
- `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:3024` — in `body()`, when parsing a method declaration (`function o:get()`), the C call `new_localvarliteral(ls, "self")` was commented out as a `TODO(port)`. `adjust_local_vars(ls, 1)` then indexed `actvar[firstlocal + 0] = actvar[1]` on a `Vec` of length 1, producing the `index out of bounds` panic.
- A second bug downstream: `:` method invocation in `suffixedexp` had `luaK_self` stubbed out, so the `funcargs` assertion `f.k == ExprKind::NonReloc` fired (the callee was still `VKStr`, never lowered through OP_SELF).

**Fix:**
- Intern `b"self"` via `state.intern_str` and call `new_local_var` before `adjust_local_vars(ls, 1)` in `body()`.
- Implemented `cg_self` (the C `luaK_self`) in `lua-parse`: discharges receiver to a register, reserves 2 regs for func+self, emits `OP_SELF` with the method name as a `K`-bit constant, and leaves `e` as `NonReloc` pointing at the base register. Wired it into the `:` arm of `suffixedexp`.

**New state:** The test program now prints `10` and exits cleanly with `status=Ok`.

result:
Output is `10` — the expected value. No instrumentation was added, so nothing to remove.

**Root cause:**
- `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:3024` — in `body()`, when parsing a method declaration (`function o:get()`), the C call `new_localvarliteral(ls, "self")` was commented out as a `TODO(port)`. `adjust_local_vars(ls, 1)` then indexed `actvar[firstlocal + 0] = actvar[1]` on a `Vec` of length 1, producing the `index out of bounds` panic.
- A second bug downstream: `:` method invocation in `suffixedexp` had `luaK_self` stubbed out, so the `funcargs` assertion `f.k == ExprKind::NonReloc` fired (the callee was still `VKStr`, never lowered through OP_SELF).

**Fix:**
- Intern `b"self"` via `state.intern_str` and call `new_local_var` before `adjust_local_vars(ls, 1)` in `body()`.
- Implemented `cg_self` (the C `luaK_self`) in `lua-parse`: discharges receiver to a register, reserves 2 regs for func+self, emits `OP_SELF` with the method name as a `K`-bit constant, and leaves `e` as `NonReloc` pointing at the base register. Wired it into the `:` arm of `suffixedexp`.

**New state:** The test program now prints `10` and exits cleanly with `status=Ok`.
